content.problemStatement:
Chef is preparing a brand new dish. He found NNN new ingredients (numbered 111 through NNN) for the dish. For each valid iii, the iii-th ingredient costs CiC_iCi‚Äã dollars and it has value ViV_iVi‚Äã.

Chef has a budget: SSS dollars. He can buy each ingredient at most once. A non-empty subset of these NNN ingredients (possibly containing all ingredients) is called affordable if the sum of costs of all the ingredients in the subset does not exceed Chef's budget. The tastiness of a dish prepared using a set of ingredients is equal to vKv^KvK, where vvv is the sum of values of all the ingredients in the set and KKK is a fixed integer.

To make a dish, Chef should use an affordable subset of ingredients. He wants to try out all the dishes he can make. Find the total (summed up) tastiness of all these dishes. Since this number could be very large, compute it modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains three space-separated integers NNN, SSS and KKK.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N), the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers CiC_iCi‚Äã and ViV_iVi‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the total tastiness modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Chef can make dishes using the following subsets of ingredients:

{1}\{1\}{1} with tastiness 22=42^2 = 422=4
{2}\{2\}{2} with tastiness 32=93^2 = 932=9
{3}\{3\}{3} with tastiness 42=164^2 = 1642=16
{1,3}\{1, 3\}{1,3} with total value 62=366^2 = 3662=36

The subsets {1,2}\{1, 2\}{1,2}, {2,3}\{2, 3\}{2,3} and {1,2,3}\{1, 2, 3\}{1,2,3} cannot be used because they are not affordable.
